name: Bloodborne systemic rejuvenation
summary: Bloodborne systemic rejuvenation is normalized for animal parabiosis and systemic-environment evidence testing whether circulating factors restore aged tissue function.
mechanism_summary: Alters the systemic blood environment to expose aged tissues or stem cells to younger circulating signals, with current linked evidence remaining preclinical.

open_questions: Whether the linked evidence generalizes beyond the specific population, model, tissue, or disease context already recorded in the public finding. Linked context: Aged HSC refractory bloodborne rejuvenation study; Aged HSCs were refractory to bloodborne rejuvenation.
Whether mechanism-specific effects can be separated from combination, product, delivery, or study-design confounding.
Whether future studies can connect the observed biomarker, functional, or model-organism signal to durable aging-relevant outcomes.
